qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SOAP cookie-to-username binding bypass permits administrator impersonation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fixes stored HTML injection in the tag deletion confirmation flow by escaping the tag name in `tag_delete.php`. This finding instead targets SOAP authentication in `api/soap/mc_api.php` and `core/authentication_api.php`, where a valid session cookie is not bound to the claimed username.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SOAP issue attachment getter bypasses private bugnote visibility checks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory's root cause is unescaped tag-name rendering in the `tag_delete.php` delete-confirmation message. This finding instead concerns missing private-bugnote authorization checks in the SOAP attachment download path under `api/soap/mc_file_api.php` and `core/file_api.php`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-fh48-f69w-7vmp is a stored HTML injection in `tag_delete.php` caused by interpolating `tag_get_name()` into the delete confirmation message without escaping, while the session's qualifying findings are unrelated SOAP authentication and SOAP attachment-authorization flaws.
evidence: The advisory patch `80990f43153167c73f11eb4b2bc7108d0c3d6b46` changes `tag_delete.php` to wrap `tag_get_name( $f_tag_id )` with `string_html_specialchars()` before calling `helper_ensure_confirmed()`, confirming the root cause is HTML injection in the tag delete confirmation path.
The run manifest lists only two final qualifying findings: `soap_cookie_username_binding_bypass` and `soap_private_bugnote_attachment_bypass`.

Those qualifying findings are rooted in SOAP code paths (`api/soap/mc_api.php`, `core/authentication_api.php`, `api/soap/mc_file_api.php`, `core/file_api.php`) and describe account impersonation or private attachment disclosure, not tag deletion or HTML rendering.
